title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: This test plan targets the top-level validation of CCF (Central Control Fabric) power management (PM) flows within the SOC. It covers coordinated power state transitions, resource arbitration, stress detection and response, license handshake and arbitration, policy-driven flow control, and system-level observability and debug. CCF integrates with PMA, Punit, and core modules to orchestrate power state changes (C-states, frequency/voltage scaling), manage exclusive access to shared resources (MLC flush, IOSF channels), and enforce system policies. Key interactions include cross-module synchronization, telemetry-driven stress handling, license negotiation, and debug visibility, ensuring robust and safe PM flow operation under normal and stress conditions.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: This test plan encompasses validation of: (1) coordinated state transitions and resource arbitration across cores/modules, including exclusive access scenarios; (2) telemetry and AI-based stress detection and response mechanisms for electrical, thermal, and power virus events; (3) license handshake and arbitration flows between PMA, Punit, and cores; (4) policy-driven flow control and ownership prioritization; (5) system-level observability and debug mechanisms for PM flows, stress events, and arbitration outcomes. The plan addresses normal operation, stress scenarios, contention, race conditions, and system recovery. It does not cover lower-level signal integrity or individual IP-specific PM flows outside CCF orchestration.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: All features require access to SOC-level debug and telemetry infrastructure, programmable stress injection capabilities, and synchronized PMA/Punit/core interfaces. Test environment must support controlled stress event generation, license arbitration triggers, and policy configuration. Observability tools must be available for real-time status monitoring and debug.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Potential security implications include unauthorized PM flow initiation, license spoofing, and denial-of-service via resource contention. Mitigations involve policy gating, license arbitration, and observability mechanisms to detect and prevent malicious or unintended transitions. Validation will ensure that security/performance policies are enforced and that flow ownership is correctly prioritized under stress and error conditions.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Risks include incomplete coverage of simultaneous stress events, false positives in stress detection, and loss of debug visibility under high contention. Contingencies involve fallback flows, enhanced observability, and additional stress scenario coverage to ensure robustness and survivability.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Prerequisites include validated PMA/Punit/core interfaces, functional telemetry and debug infrastructure, and programmable policy and license configuration. Stress injection tools and observability mechanisms must be operational prior to test execution.

title: License Handshake and Safe Transition Arbitration
subsection content: Validate ICCP/PVP license handshakes and arbitration across PMA, Punit, and cores. Trigger overlapping and conflicting license requests, ensuring only dominant licenses are granted, transitions are synchronized, and safe work-points are applied. Check for correct handling of license revocation and transition aborts under stress.

title: Overlapping and Conflicting ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Validate that the arbitration logic correctly handles overlapping and conflicting ICCP license requests from both cores, always granting the maximum requested license and ensuring no deadlock or starvation occurs.

title: Overlapping and Conflicting ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Randomly generate simultaneous or closely sequenced ICCP license requests from both cores with overlapping and conflicting values, and observe arbitration and grant behavior.

title: Overlapping and Conflicting ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Randomize ICCP[3:0] values (0-4 for clients, 1-4 for servers) and the timing of requests from both cores to create overlapping and conflicting scenarios.

title: Overlapping and Conflicting ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Both cores must be active and out of C6; ICCP request and grant signals must be observable; GV CTRL and PMA arbitration logic must be accessible for monitoring.

title: Overlapping and Conflicting ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Involves cores, PMA ICCP arbitration, GV CTRL, and TADREG for request handling and grant signaling.

title: Overlapping and Conflicting ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Ensure no deadlock, starvation, or dropped requests except when superseded by a higher request within the hysteresis window; monitor for correct arbitration and survivability.

title: Simultaneous Overlapping ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: This test configures both cores to issue ICCP license requests with randomized legal values at the same time or in close succession, creating overlapping and conflicting scenarios. The arbitration logic is observed to ensure that the maximum requested license is always granted, and only the dominant license is applied. The test also checks that no deadlock or starvation occurs and that requests are not dropped unless superseded by a higher request within the hysteresis window.

title: Simultaneous Overlapping ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active and out of C6. 2. Randomly select ICCP[3:0] values for each core within the legal range (0-4 for clients, 1-4 for servers). 3. Simultaneously or with minimal offset, set C2PmICCPRequestMXnnnH.Valid=1 and C2PmICCPRequestMXnnnH.ICCP[3:0] for both cores. 4. Monitor ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH signals to each core and the selected license in GV CTRL (pma_iccp, pma_gv_ctrl). 5. Repeat with various overlapping/conflicting value combinations. 6. Log arbitration outcomes and check for deadlock or starvation.

title: Simultaneous Overlapping ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Pass if the maximum requested license is always granted, only the dominant license is applied, and no deadlock or starvation occurs. Fail if a lower license is granted when a higher is requested, if both licenses are granted, or if deadlock/starvation is observed. HW errors include missed grants, incorrect GV CTRL state, or dropped requests outside the hysteresis window.

title: Simultaneous Overlapping ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Use Visa and PMON to sample ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH and GV CTRL registers. Check logs for arbitration results and absence of deadlock/starvation.

title: Simultaneous Overlapping ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for ICCP arbitration and grant logic.

title: Simultaneous Overlapping ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: Use OSBV/SVOS with latest validation stack, PMON for register sampling, Perspec for scenario generation, PythonSV for scripting, and Supercollider for stress. Map OS-level utilities for concurrency and register checks.

title: Simultaneous Overlapping ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with ICCP arbitration support, correct OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated PCIe/storage/graphics drivers, and required registry keys for ICCP observability.

title: Simultaneous Overlapping ICCP License Requests Arbitration
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with two active cores, correct SoC stepping, full DIMM and PCIe population, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Conflicting ICCP License Requests with Hysteresis Window
subsection content: This test issues ICCP license requests from both cores with conflicting values, focusing on scenarios where a lower request is made shortly after a higher one, within the hysteresis window. The test verifies that the arbitration logic does not grant the lower license until the hysteresis condition is met and that only the dominant license is applied.

title: Conflicting ICCP License Requests with Hysteresis Window
subsection content: 1. Activate both cores and ensure they are out of C6. 2. Core0 issues a high ICCP license request (e.g., License=4), Core1 issues a lower request (e.g., License=2) within the hysteresis window. 3. Set Valid=1 for both requests. 4. Monitor ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH and GV CTRL for grant behavior. 5. Repeat with varying timing and value combinations to cover hysteresis logic. 6. Log arbitration and grant timing.

title: Conflicting ICCP License Requests with Hysteresis Window
subsection content: Pass if the lower license is not granted until the hysteresis window expires and only the dominant license is applied. Fail if the lower license is granted prematurely or both licenses are granted. HW errors include incorrect hysteresis handling or grant timing.

title: Conflicting ICCP License Requests with Hysteresis Window
subsection content: Use PMON and Visa to monitor grant signals and GV CTRL. Analyze logs for correct hysteresis behavior.

title: Conflicting ICCP License Requests with Hysteresis Window
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for hysteresis and arbitration timing.

title: Conflicting ICCP License Requests with Hysteresis Window
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use concurrency and timing utilities for hysteresis coverage.

title: Conflicting ICCP License Requests with Hysteresis Window
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with hysteresis logic enabled, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, and registry keys for grant timing.

title: Conflicting ICCP License Requests with Hysteresis Window

subsection content: Two-core platform, correct SoC stepping, full DIMM/PCIe, thermal solution, debug and power measurement setup.

title: No Deadlock or Starvation under Rapid ICCP Requests
subsection content: This test rapidly issues ICCP license requests from both cores with randomized values and timing to stress the arbitration logic. It verifies that the system does not enter deadlock or starvation, and that all requests are eventually processed according to the arbitration rules.

title: No Deadlock or Starvation under Rapid ICCP Requests
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active and out of C6. 2. Rapidly and repeatedly randomize ICCP[3:0] values and Valid signals for both cores. 3. Monitor ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH and GV CTRL for grant behavior and request processing. 4. Check for any unprocessed requests, deadlock, or starvation. 5. Repeat for extended cycles to ensure robustness.

title: No Deadlock or Starvation under Rapid ICCP Requests
subsection content: Pass if all requests are eventually processed, no deadlock or starvation occurs, and arbitration always grants the maximum requested license. Fail if requests are lost, deadlock/starvation is observed, or incorrect grants occur. HW errors include missed grants or stuck arbitration.

title: No Deadlock or Starvation under Rapid ICCP Requests
subsection content: Use Visa, PMON, and logs to monitor request and grant signals, arbitration state, and system liveness.

title: No Deadlock or Starvation under Rapid ICCP Requests
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for request/grant liveness.

title: No Deadlock or Starvation under Rapid ICCP Requests
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use stress and concurrency tools for rapid request generation.

title: No Deadlock or Starvation under Rapid ICCP Requests
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with arbitration and liveness support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for stress testing.

title: No Deadlock or Starvation under Rapid ICCP Requests

title: Synchronized Safe Transition and Work-Point Application on License Change
subsection content: Validate that ICCP license changes trigger safe and synchronized work-point transitions, with correct AGV and grant sequencing for both up and down transitions.

title: Synchronized Safe Transition and Work-Point Application on License Change
subsection content: Randomly trigger ICCP license changes from one or both cores, ensuring correct sequencing of AGV and grant signals, and monitoring work-point application.

title: Synchronized Safe Transition and Work-Point Application on License Change
subsection content: Randomize license change direction (up/down), core selection, and ICCP[3:0] values for each transition.

title: Synchronized Safe Transition and Work-Point Application on License Change
subsection content: Both cores active with valid work-points, GV CTRL not in GO Inc/Dec phase, Rcsm/Dispatcher ready, and Acode WP calculation available.

title: Synchronized Safe Transition and Work-Point Application on License Change
subsection content: Involves cores, PMA, GV CTRL, AGV, TADREG, and Acode for work-point calculation and transition sequencing.

title: Synchronized Safe Transition and Work-Point Application on License Change
subsection content: Ensure atomicity and synchronization of transitions, monitor for unsafe or intermediate states, and validate correct grant and AGV order.

title: ICCP License Up Transition with Safe AGV Sequencing
subsection content: This test triggers an ICCP license up request from one core, ensuring that the AGV (voltage/frequency up) transition is completed before the license grant is sent. The test verifies that the correct work-point is applied atomically and that no unsafe or intermediate state is observed.

title: ICCP License Up Transition with Safe AGV Sequencing
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active with valid work-points. 2. Confirm GV CTRL is not in GO Inc/Dec phase (GoIncDecPhaseX4H==0) and Rcsm/Dispatcher is ready (AgvRCSMReadyX4H=1). 3. Randomly select a core and issue an ICCP license up request (C2PmICCPRequestMXnnnH.Valid=1, ICCP[3:0] incremented). 4. Simulate Acode WP calculation if needed. 5. Monitor AGV command issuance, WP selection, and timing of ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH grant relative to AGV. 6. Log the sequence and check for atomic application.

title: ICCP License Up Transition with Safe AGV Sequencing
subsection content: Pass if AGV transition completes before the grant is sent, the correct work-point is applied atomically, and no unsafe/intermediate state is observed. Fail if grant is sent before AGV or if an unsafe state occurs. HW errors include incorrect sequencing or partial WP application.

title: ICCP License Up Transition with Safe AGV Sequencing
subsection content: Use Visa and PMON to monitor AGV, grant, and WP registers. Analyze logs for correct sequencing and atomicity.

title: ICCP License Up Transition with Safe AGV Sequencing
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for AGV and grant sequencing.

title: ICCP License Up Transition with Safe AGV Sequencing
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use scenario generation and register monitoring tools.

title: ICCP License Up Transition with Safe AGV Sequencing
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with AGV and grant sequencing support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for WP and AGV observability.

title: ICCP License Up Transition with Safe AGV Sequencing

title: ICCP License Down Transition with Safe Grant and AGV Order
subsection content: This test triggers an ICCP license down request from one core, ensuring that the license grant is sent before the AGV (voltage/frequency down) transition. The test verifies that the correct work-point is applied atomically and that no unsafe or intermediate state is observed.

title: ICCP License Down Transition with Safe Grant and AGV Order
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active with valid work-points. 2. Confirm GV CTRL is not in GO Inc/Dec phase and Rcsm/Dispatcher is ready. 3. Randomly select a core and issue an ICCP license down request (C2PmICCPRequestMXnnnH.Valid=1, ICCP[3:0] decremented). 4. Simulate Acode WP calculation if needed. 5. Monitor timing of ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH grant and AGV command issuance. 6. Log the sequence and check for atomic application.

title: ICCP License Down Transition with Safe Grant and AGV Order
subsection content: Pass if the grant is sent before AGV, the correct work-point is applied atomically, and no unsafe/intermediate state is observed. Fail if AGV occurs before grant or if an unsafe state occurs. HW errors include incorrect sequencing or partial WP application.

title: ICCP License Down Transition with Safe Grant and AGV Order
subsection content: Use PMON and Visa to monitor grant and AGV signals, and WP registers. Analyze logs for correct sequencing.

title: ICCP License Down Transition with Safe Grant and AGV Order
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for grant and AGV order.

title: ICCP License Down Transition with Safe Grant and AGV Order
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use scenario generation and register monitoring tools.

title: ICCP License Down Transition with Safe Grant and AGV Order
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with grant and AGV sequencing support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for WP and AGV observability.

title: ICCP License Down Transition with Safe Grant and AGV Order

title: Concurrent ICCP License Changes from Both Cores
subsection content: This test triggers ICCP license changes (up and down) from both cores in close succession, ensuring that the system correctly sequences AGV and grant signals for each transition and applies the correct work-points atomically without unsafe states.

title: Concurrent ICCP License Changes from Both Cores
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active with valid work-points. 2. Confirm GV CTRL is not in GO Inc/Dec phase and Rcsm/Dispatcher is ready. 3. Randomly trigger ICCP license changes (up and down) from both cores using C2PmICCPRequestMXnnnH.Valid=1 and ICCP[3:0]. 4. Simulate Acode WP calculation as needed. 5. Monitor AGV, grant, and WP application for each core. 6. Log all transitions and check for atomicity and safety.

title: Concurrent ICCP License Changes from Both Cores
subsection content: Pass if all transitions are sequenced correctly (AGV before grant for up, grant before AGV for down), correct work-points are applied atomically, and no unsafe/intermediate state is observed. Fail if sequencing is incorrect or unsafe states occur. HW errors include missed transitions or partial WP application.

title: Concurrent ICCP License Changes from Both Cores
subsection content: Use Visa, PMON, and logs to monitor all transitions, AGV, grant, and WP registers.

title: Concurrent ICCP License Changes from Both Cores
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for concurrent transitions.

title: Concurrent ICCP License Changes from Both Cores
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use concurrency and scenario generation tools.

title: Concurrent ICCP License Changes from Both Cores
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with concurrent transition support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for WP and AGV observability.

title: License Revocation and Transition Abort under Stress
subsection content: Validate that the system safely aborts ongoing ICCP transitions and retains the last valid work-point and license when break events occur under stress, and that only the latest license request is eventually granted.

title: License Revocation and Transition Abort under Stress
subsection content: Randomly trigger rapid ICCP license requests from both cores and inject break events during transitions to observe abort and recovery behavior.

title: License Revocation and Transition Abort under Stress
subsection content: Randomize ICCP license request values, timing, and break event injection (throttle hammer, CPD, thermal) during transitions.

title: License Revocation and Transition Abort under Stress
subsection content: Both cores active with valid work-points, break event injection capability, and observability of grant, AGV, and break reason/status registers.

title: License Revocation and Transition Abort under Stress
subsection content: Involves cores, PMA, AGV, TADREG, break event logic, and work-point management.

title: License Revocation and Transition Abort under Stress
subsection content: Ensure correct abort and recovery behavior, monitor for unsafe or partial work-point application, and validate break reason updates.

title: Transition Abort on Break Event during ICCP License Change
subsection content: This test triggers an ICCP license transition and injects a break event (throttle hammer, CPD, or thermal) during the transition. It verifies that the system aborts the transition, retains the last valid work-point and license, and updates the break reason accordingly. No unsafe or partial work-point should be applied.

title: Transition Abort on Break Event during ICCP License Change
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active with valid work-points. 2. Trigger an ICCP license change (up or down) from one core. 3. During the transition, inject a break event (throttle hammer, CPD, or thermal). 4. Monitor ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH, AGV state, and break reason/status registers. 5. Log the abort behavior and check for retention of last valid work-point and license.

title: Transition Abort on Break Event during ICCP License Change
subsection content: Pass if the transition is aborted, the last valid work-point and license are retained, and the break reason is updated. Fail if an unsafe or partial work-point is applied or if the break reason is not updated. HW errors include incorrect abort handling or state retention.

title: Transition Abort on Break Event during ICCP License Change
subsection content: Use Visa and PMON to monitor AGV, grant, and break reason/status registers. Analyze logs for abort and recovery behavior.

title: Transition Abort on Break Event during ICCP License Change
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for abort and recovery flows.

title: Transition Abort on Break Event during ICCP License Change
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use scenario generation and break event injection tools.

title: Transition Abort on Break Event during ICCP License Change
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with break event handling support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for break reason observability.

title: Rapid ICCP License Requests with Overlapping Transitions
subsection content: This test rapidly triggers ICCP license requests from both cores, including cases where a new request arrives before the previous transition completes. It verifies that only the latest request is eventually granted after safe completion or abort of the current transition, and that no unsafe or partial work-point is applied.

title: Rapid ICCP License Requests with Overlapping Transitions
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active with valid work-points. 2. Rapidly and repeatedly trigger ICCP license requests (up and down) from both cores with randomized timing. 3. Monitor ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH, AGV state, and work-point application. 4. Check that only the latest request is eventually granted after safe completion or abort of ongoing transitions. 5. Log all transitions and outcomes.

title: Rapid ICCP License Requests with Overlapping Transitions
subsection content: Pass if only the latest request is granted after safe completion or abort, and no unsafe/partial work-point is applied. Fail if earlier requests are granted out of order or unsafe states occur. HW errors include missed grants or incorrect state transitions.

title: Rapid ICCP License Requests with Overlapping Transitions
subsection content: Use PMON and Visa to monitor grant, AGV, and work-point registers. Analyze logs for correct request handling.

title: Rapid ICCP License Requests with Overlapping Transitions
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for rapid transition handling.

title: Rapid ICCP License Requests with Overlapping Transitions
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use stress and concurrency tools for rapid request generation.

title: Rapid ICCP License Requests with Overlapping Transitions
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with rapid transition support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for transition observability.

title: Break Reason Update and State Retention Verification
subsection content: This test injects break events during ICCP transitions and verifies that the break reason/status registers are updated correctly and that the last valid work-point and license are retained. It ensures that no unsafe or partial work-point is applied.

title: Break Reason Update and State Retention Verification
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active with valid work-points. 2. Trigger an ICCP license transition from one core. 3. Inject a break event (throttle hammer, CPD, or thermal) during the transition. 4. Monitor break reason/status registers and work-point/license state. 5. Log updates and check for correct state retention.

title: Break Reason Update and State Retention Verification
subsection content: Pass if break reason/status registers are updated and last valid work-point/license are retained. Fail if registers are not updated or state is lost. HW errors include incorrect break reason or state retention.

title: Break Reason Update and State Retention Verification
subsection content: Use Visa and PMON to monitor break reason/status and work-point/license registers. Analyze logs for correct updates.

title: Break Reason Update and State Retention Verification
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with break reason/status support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for break reason observability.

title: Safe Work-Point Application and Synchronization with GO Command
subsection content: Validate that the core/IP applies new WP4 work-points only after receiving the corresponding GO_INC or GO_DEC command, ensuring atomic and synchronized transitions with correct frequency limits per license level.

title: Safe Work-Point Application and Synchronization with GO Command
subsection content: Randomly trigger WP4 writes (WP4_LOW, WP4_MEDIUM, WP4_HIGH) with GO_INC or GO_DEC commands and monitor work-point application and synchronization.

title: Safe Work-Point Application and Synchronization with GO Command
subsection content: Randomize WP4 write values and the timing/order of GO_INC/GO_DEC commands to test synchronization and atomicity.

title: Safe Work-Point Application and Synchronization with GO Command
subsection content: ACP mode enabled, Pcode sends resolved TRL rows as WP4 writes, and GO_INC/GO_DEC command capability.

title: Safe Work-Point Application and Synchronization with GO Command
subsection content: Involves core/IP, Pcode, WP4 registers, GO command logic, and frequency limit management.

title: Safe Work-Point Application and Synchronization with GO Command
subsection content: Ensure atomic and synchronized application of work-points, monitor for correct frequency limits and absence of intermediate states.

title: WP4 Write and GO_INC Command Synchronization
subsection content: This test writes new WP4 values (WP4_LOW, WP4_MEDIUM, WP4_HIGH) to the core/IP and issues a GO_INC command. It verifies that the core/IP does not apply the new work-point until the GO_INC command is received and that the transition is atomic and synchronized.

title: WP4 Write and GO_INC Command Synchronization
subsection content: 1. Enable ACP mode and ensure Pcode has sent resolved TRL rows as WP4 writes. 2. Write new values to WP4_LOW, WP4_MEDIUM, and WP4_HIGH registers. 3. Issue a GO_INC command to the core. 4. Monitor work-point application and frequency limits. 5. Log the timing and synchronization of the transition.

title: WP4 Write and GO_INC Command Synchronization
subsection content: Pass if the new work-point is applied only after GO_INC is received, the transition is atomic, and the correct frequency limit is applied. Fail if the work-point is applied before GO_INC or if an intermediate state is observed. HW errors include incorrect synchronization or frequency limit application.

title: WP4 Write and GO_INC Command Synchronization
subsection content: Use Visa and PMON to monitor WP4 registers, GO_INC command, and frequency limits. Analyze logs for synchronization and atomicity.

title: WP4 Write and GO_INC Command Synchronization
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for WP4 and GO command synchronization.

subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use register monitoring and scenario generation tools.

title: WP4 Write and GO_INC Command Synchronization
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with WP4 and GO command support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for WP4 observability.

title: WP4 Write and GO_DEC Command Synchronization
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with ACP mode enabled, correct SoC stepping, full DIMM/PCIe, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Frequency Limit Verification per License Level after WP4 Application
subsection content: This test verifies that after applying new WP4 values with GO_INC or GO_DEC commands, the core/IP applies the correct frequency limits per license level as resolved by Pcode. It ensures that the applied frequency matches the expected TRL for the granted license.

title: Frequency Limit Verification per License Level after WP4 Application
subsection content: 1. Enable ACP mode and ensure Pcode has sent resolved TRL rows as WP4 writes. 2. Write new WP4 values and issue GO_INC or GO_DEC commands. 3. Monitor the applied frequency and compare it to the expected TRL for the granted license. 4. Log the results and check for mismatches.

title: Frequency Limit Verification per License Level after WP4 Application
subsection content: Pass if the applied frequency matches the resolved TRL for the granted license after WP4 application. Fail if there is a mismatch or incorrect frequency limit. HW errors include incorrect frequency application or TRL mismatch.

title: Frequency Limit Verification per License Level after WP4 Application
subsection content: Use Visa and PMON to monitor applied frequency, WP4 registers, and TRL values. Analyze logs for correct frequency limit application.

title: Frequency Limit Verification per License Level after WP4 Application
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for frequency limit verification.

title: Frequency Limit Verification per License Level after WP4 Application
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use frequency monitoring and scenario generation tools.

title: Frequency Limit Verification per License Level after WP4 Application
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with TRL and WP4 support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for frequency observability.

title: Frequency Limit Verification per License Level after WP4 Application
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with ACP mode enabled, correct SoC stepping, full DIMM/PCIe, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: ICCP/PVP Grant and Threshold Propagation and Observability
subsection content: Validate that ICCP license grants correctly propagate PVP threshold values, and that grant and threshold signals are updated and observable at both core and PMA interfaces with no mismatches or stale values.

title: ICCP/PVP Grant and Threshold Propagation and Observability
subsection content: Randomly trigger ICCP license requests and monitor the propagation and update of ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH, PVP1024Thold[7:0], and PvpThold[7:0] signals at all relevant interfaces.

title: ICCP/PVP Grant and Threshold Propagation and Observability
subsection content: Randomize ICCP license request values and timing to cover all grant and threshold update scenarios.

title: ICCP/PVP Grant and Threshold Propagation and Observability
subsection content: Both cores active with valid work-points, observability of grant and threshold signals at core and PMA interfaces.

title: ICCP/PVP Grant and Threshold Propagation and Observability
subsection content: Involves cores, PMA, TADREG, PVP threshold logic, and grant signaling.

title: ICCP/PVP Grant and Threshold Propagation and Observability
subsection content: Ensure valid and updated propagation of grant and threshold signals, monitor for mismatches or stale values.

title: ICCP License Grant and PVP Threshold Update Verification
subsection content: This test triggers ICCP license requests from both cores and verifies that for each grant, the ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH, PVP1024Thold[7:0], and PvpThold[7:0] signals are updated and match the expected values for the granted license. It ensures that no mismatches or stale values are observed.

title: ICCP License Grant and PVP Threshold Update Verification
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active with valid work-points. 2. Randomly trigger ICCP license requests from both cores. 3. For each grant, monitor ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH, PVP1024Thold[7:0], and PvpThold[7:0] at core and PMA interfaces. 4. Compare observed values to expected values for the granted license. 5. Log all updates and check for mismatches or stale values.

title: ICCP License Grant and PVP Threshold Update Verification
subsection content: Pass if all signals are updated and match expected values for each grant, with no mismatches or stale values. Fail if any signal is incorrect or not updated. HW errors include mismatched or stale grant/threshold signals.

title: ICCP License Grant and PVP Threshold Update Verification
subsection content: Use Visa and PMON to monitor grant and threshold signals at all interfaces. Analyze logs for correct propagation and updates.

title: ICCP License Grant and PVP Threshold Update Verification
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for grant and threshold propagation.

title: ICCP License Grant and PVP Threshold Update Verification
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use register monitoring and scenario generation tools.

title: ICCP License Grant and PVP Threshold Update Verification
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with grant and threshold propagation support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for signal observability.

title: ICCP License Grant and PVP Threshold Update Verification
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with two active cores, correct SoC stepping, full DIMM/PCIe, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Stale Value Detection on Grant and Threshold Signals
subsection content: This test triggers ICCP license requests and monitors for stale values on ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH, PVP1024Thold[7:0], and PvpThold[7:0] signals. It verifies that all signals are updated promptly with each grant and that no old or incorrect values persist.

title: Stale Value Detection on Grant and Threshold Signals
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active with valid work-points. 2. Trigger ICCP license requests with varying values and timing. 3. Monitor grant and threshold signals at core and PMA interfaces. 4. Check for any stale or outdated values after each grant. 5. Log all observations and analyze for prompt updates.

title: Stale Value Detection on Grant and Threshold Signals
subsection content: Pass if all signals are updated promptly with each grant and no stale values are observed. Fail if any signal retains an old value after a new grant. HW errors include stale or delayed signal updates.

title: Stale Value Detection on Grant and Threshold Signals
subsection content: Use PMON and Visa to monitor signal updates and logs for stale value detection.

title: Stale Value Detection on Grant and Threshold Signals
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for signal update timing.

title: Stale Value Detection on Grant and Threshold Signals
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use register monitoring and scenario generation tools.

title: Stale Value Detection on Grant and Threshold Signals
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with signal update support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for signal observability.

title: Stale Value Detection on Grant and Threshold Signals
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with two active cores, correct SoC stepping, full DIMM/PCIe, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Grant and Threshold Signal Consistency across Core and PMA Interfaces
subsection content: This test verifies that ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH, PVP1024Thold[7:0], and PvpThold[7:0] signals are consistent and match at both the core and PMA interfaces for each ICCP license grant. It ensures that no mismatches occur between interfaces.

title: Grant and Threshold Signal Consistency across Core and PMA Interfaces
subsection content: 1. Ensure both cores are active with valid work-points. 2. Trigger ICCP license requests and monitor grant and threshold signals at both core and PMA interfaces. 3. Compare values at both interfaces for each grant. 4. Log all comparisons and check for mismatches.

title: Grant and Threshold Signal Consistency across Core and PMA Interfaces
subsection content: Pass if all signals are consistent and match at both interfaces for each grant. Fail if any mismatch is observed. HW errors include inconsistent signal values between interfaces.

title: Grant and Threshold Signal Consistency across Core and PMA Interfaces
subsection content: Use Visa and PMON to monitor and compare signals at both interfaces. Analyze logs for consistency.

title: Grant and Threshold Signal Consistency across Core and PMA Interfaces
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for signal consistency.

title: Grant and Threshold Signal Consistency across Core and PMA Interfaces
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS, PMON, Perspec, PythonSV, Supercollider. Use register monitoring and scenario generation tools.

title: Grant and Threshold Signal Consistency across Core and PMA Interfaces
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with signal consistency support, OS kernel and scheduler settings, validated drivers, registry keys for signal observability.

title: Grant and Threshold Signal Consistency across Core and PMA Interfaces

subsection content: Platform with two active cores, correct SoC stepping, full DIMM/PCIe, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.
